title: HardInterventionOn

tex_statement.raw:
% Definition file for row def_3_10 -- "HardInterventionOn".
% Auto-generated stub by scaffold/solving_current_row.py at row start. The
% manager agent (or a worker dispatched by it) fills the body below with the
% Lean-faithful definition statement(s). Stays close to the lecture notes.
%
% Compiles standalone via the `subfiles` package -- the parent main.tex in
% this folder provides the preamble.

\documentclass[main]{subfiles}

\begin{document}
% ref: def_3_10
% title: HardInterventionOn
\def\rowref{def\_3\_10}
\phantomsection\label{def_3_10}

\begin{Def}[Hard intervention on CDMGs]\label{def:G_hard_intervention}
    Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG and $W \ins J \cup V$ a subset of nodes.
  The \emph{intervened CDMG} w.r.t.\ $W$ of $G$ is the CDMG: 
  \[ G_{\doit(W)}:=(J_{\doit(W)},V_{\doit(W)},E_{\doit(W)},L_{\doit(W)}),\] 
  %\[G(V\sm W|\doit(W \cup J)):=G_{\doit(W)}:=(J_{\doit(W)},V_{\doit(W)},E_{\doit(W)},L_{\doit(W)}),\] 
  where:
  \begin{enumerate}[label=\roman*.)]
      \item $J_{\doit(W)}:= J \cup W$,
      \item $V_{\doit(W)}:= V \sm W$,
      \item $E_{\doit(W)}:= E \sm \lC v \tuh w \,|\, v \in G, w  \in W  \rC$,
      \item $L_{\doit(W)}:= L \sm \lC v \huh w\,|\, v \in G, w \in W \rC$,
  \end{enumerate}
  where we turn all nodes from $W$ into input nodes and remove all edges into nodes from $W$.
\end{Def}

\end{document}
tex_statement.html:
Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG and $W \ins J \cup V$ a subset of nodes. The intervened CDMG w.r.t.\ $W$ of $G$ is the CDMG: \[ G_{\doit(W)}:=(J_{\doit(W)},V_{\doit(W)},E_{\doit(W)},L_{\doit(W)}),\]

where:

  1. $J_{\doit(W)}:= J \cup W$,
  2. $V_{\doit(W)}:= V \sm W$,
  3. $E_{\doit(W)}:= E \sm \lC v \tuh w \,|\, v \in G, w \in W \rC$,
  4. $L_{\doit(W)}:= L \sm \lC v \huh w\,|\, v \in G, w \in W \rC$,

where we turn all nodes from $W$ into input nodes and remove all edges into nodes from $W$.

lean_code_with_comments:
/-- The *hard intervention* of the CDMG `G` with respect to a set
of nodes `W ⊆ α`: the new CDMG `G_{do(W)}` obtained by promoting
every vertex of `W` to an input node, removing every directed
edge whose *target* lies in `W`, and removing every bidirected
edge incident to *any* vertex of `W`. See
`lecture-notes/lecture_notes/graphs.tex` definition
`def:G_hard_intervention` (def 3.10 of the LN).

This is intentionally well-defined for *every* `W : Set α`, with
no `W ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V` precondition -- see the design note above for
why (the iterated form `(G.hardInterventionOn W₁).hardInterventionOn
W₂` of claim_3_4 needs this generality). The four `@[simp]` lemmas
`hardInterventionOn_J`, `hardInterventionOn_V`,
`mem_hardInterventionOn_E`, `mem_hardInterventionOn_L` below
characterise the four components of the result. -/
def hardInterventionOn (G : CDMG α) (W : Set α) : CDMG α where
  J := G.J ∪ W
  V := G.V \ W
  disjoint_JV := by
    rw [Set.disjoint_left]
    rintro x (hJ | hW) ⟨hV, hnW⟩
    · exact Set.disjoint_left.mp G.disjoint_JV hJ hV
    · exact hnW hW
  E := G.E \ { p : α × α | p.2 ∈ W }
  E_subset := by
    rintro ⟨v, w⟩ ⟨hE, hnW⟩
    obtain ⟨hv, hw⟩ := G.E_subset hE
    refine ⟨?_, hw, hnW⟩
    rcases hv with hJ | hV
    · exact Or.inl (Or.inl hJ)
    · by_cases hW : v ∈ W
      · exact Or.inl (Or.inr hW)
      · exact Or.inr ⟨hV, hW⟩
  L := G.L \ { p : α × α | p.1 ∈ W ∨ p.2 ∈ W }
  L_subset := by
    rintro ⟨v₁, v₂⟩ ⟨hL, hnW⟩
    obtain ⟨h1, h2⟩ := G.L_subset hL
    exact ⟨⟨h1, fun hW => hnW (Or.inl hW)⟩, ⟨h2, fun hW => hnW (Or.inr hW)⟩⟩
  L_irrefl := by
    intro v₁ v₂ h
    exact G.L_irrefl h.1
  L_symm := by
    intro v₁ v₂ h
    exact ⟨G.L_symm h.1, fun hh => h.2 hh.symm⟩
  disjoint_EL := by
    rw [Set.disjoint_left]
    rintro p ⟨hE, _⟩ ⟨hL, _⟩
    exact Set.disjoint_left.mp G.disjoint_EL hE hL
lean_code_without_comments:
def hardInterventionOn (G : CDMG α) (W : Set α) : CDMG α where
  J := G.J ∪ W
  V := G.V \ W
  disjoint_JV := by
    rw [Set.disjoint_left]
    rintro x (hJ | hW) ⟨hV, hnW⟩
    · exact Set.disjoint_left.mp G.disjoint_JV hJ hV
    · exact hnW hW
  E := G.E \ { p : α × α | p.2 ∈ W }
  E_subset := by
    rintro ⟨v, w⟩ ⟨hE, hnW⟩
    obtain ⟨hv, hw⟩ := G.E_subset hE
    refine ⟨?_, hw, hnW⟩
    rcases hv with hJ | hV
    · exact Or.inl (Or.inl hJ)
    · by_cases hW : v ∈ W
      · exact Or.inl (Or.inr hW)
      · exact Or.inr ⟨hV, hW⟩
  L := G.L \ { p : α × α | p.1 ∈ W ∨ p.2 ∈ W }
  L_subset := by
    rintro ⟨v₁, v₂⟩ ⟨hL, hnW⟩
    obtain ⟨h1, h2⟩ := G.L_subset hL
    exact ⟨⟨h1, fun hW => hnW (Or.inl hW)⟩, ⟨h2, fun hW => hnW (Or.inr hW)⟩⟩
  L_irrefl := by
    intro v₁ v₂ h
    exact G.L_irrefl h.1
  L_symm := by
    intro v₁ v₂ h
    exact ⟨G.L_symm h.1, fun hh => h.2 hh.symm⟩
  disjoint_EL := by
    rw [Set.disjoint_left]
    rintro p ⟨hE, _⟩ ⟨hL, _⟩
    exact Set.disjoint_left.mp G.disjoint_EL hE hL
lean_explanation:
The Lean definition takes two arguments: a CDMG `G : CDMG α` over an ambient vertex type `α : Type*`, and a subset `W : Set α` of that type. It produces a fresh `CDMG α` whose four data fields are direct transcriptions of the LN's four bullets — $J \cup W$, $V \setminus W$, the trimmed $E$, the trimmed $L$ — and then discharges the seven structural obligations inherited from the `CDMG` structure of def 3.1. The dot-projection `G.hardInterventionOn W` is read as "the hard intervention of $G$ on $W$" and is the Lean spelling of the LN's subscript $G_{\doit(W)}$.

Most of the Mathlib idioms are standard set theory. `G.J ∪ W` is `Set.union`, matching $J \cup W$. `G.V \ W` is `Set.diff`, matching $V \setminus W$. The directed edges become `G.E \ { p : α × α | p.2 ∈ W }`: a `Set (α × α)` from which we delete every pair whose *second* component (the target) lies in `W`. The bidirected edges become `G.L \ { p : α × α | p.1 ∈ W ∨ p.2 ∈ W }`: every ordered pair with *either* endpoint in `W` is deleted (the design notes below explain why this disjunction rather than just `p.2 ∈ W`).

The seven structural obligations are discharged inline within the `where` clause. `disjoint_JV` is rewritten through `Set.disjoint_left` and dispatched by case-splitting on which side of `G.J ∪ W` a hypothetical common vertex lies in. `E_subset` reproves that every surviving directed edge has its endpoints in the new $J \cup V$, with a three-case split on the source vertex `v` (in `G.J`, in `G.V ∩ W`, or in `G.V \ W`). `L_subset`, `L_irrefl`, `L_symm`, and `disjoint_EL` each reduce in one or two lines to the corresponding `G.*` field plus a set-membership manipulation: the deletions only shrink each set, so monotone fields lift directly, and the both-endpoints exclusion in `L` makes the deleted set itself symmetric so `L_symm` survives.

In short: the definition bundles four data fields (the four LN bullets) and seven proof fields (the CDMG structural obligations), with no new notation and no preconditions on `W`.
design_choices:
**No `W ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V` precondition.** The LN literally writes "let $W \subseteq J \cup V$", but the four set-theoretic formulas are well-defined for *any* `W : Set α`. A spurious node in $W \setminus (J \cup V)$ simply gets added to the input set without touching any edges — no directed or bidirected edge of `G` touches such a node, by `G.E_subset` and `G.L_subset`. We drop the precondition because (i) the iterated form `(G.hardInterventionOn W₁).hardInterventionOn W₂` would otherwise need a separate `W₂ ⊆ (G_{do(W₁)}).J ∪ (G_{do(W₁)}).V` hypothesis on the inner intervention, which the LN's claim 3.4 never re-proves; and (ii) every downstream user — single-vertex bifurcation claims, disjoint intervention claims, the do-calculus and identification machinery of later chapters — is one hypothesis shorter. The trade-off is that, for a `W` not contained in $J \cup V$, the spurious nodes appear as inputs of the result; in practice every *claim* that reasons about $G_{\doit(W)}$ in detail adds the precondition at the claim level, exactly as the LN does informally.

**Both-endpoints exclusion for bidirected edges.** The LN writes $L \setminus \{ v \leftrightarrow w \mid v \in G,\, w \in W \}$ with unordered $\leftrightarrow$. Internally we store bidirected edges as ordered pairs in `Set (α × α)` together with a symmetry field `L_symm`. If we deleted only pairs whose *second* component lies in `W`, a pair `(a, b) ∈ G.L` with `a ∉ W` and `b ∈ W` would lose `(a, b)` but keep its mirror `(b, a)`, breaking `L_symm` on the result. We therefore delete every pair with *either* endpoint in `W`: this matches the LN's "remove all edges into nodes from `W`" prose under the unordered reading of $\leftrightarrow$, and makes the deleted set itself symmetric so the new `L` inherits symmetry exactly.

**Drop the redundant `v ∈ G` clause for directed edges.** The LN writes $E \setminus \{ v \to w \mid v \in G,\, w \in W \}$, but every directed edge of `G.E` already has its source in $J \cup V = G$ by `G.E_subset`. The two formulations agree as subsets of `G.E`, so we use the cleaner `G.E \ { p | p.2 ∈ W }`. The bidirected clause keeps its disjunction (rather than `p.2 ∈ W` alone) for the symmetry reason above.

**Plain prose name, no new notation.** The LN macro `\doit(W)` reads as a subscript $G_{\doit(W)}$. The Lean identifier `hardInterventionOn` is searchable and pronounceable, and the dot-projection `G.hardInterventionOn W` lines up with the LN phrasing "the hard intervention of $G$ on $W$". Introducing dedicated `notation` syntax at this row would commit to a token and precedence before any downstream proof exists to motivate the choice; a later row can add infix syntax if the prose form proves clunky in practice.

**Structural fields discharged in place.** Each of the seven CDMG obligations is a one- to three-line consequence of the corresponding `G.*` field plus a set-membership manipulation, so we discharge them within the `where` clause rather than splitting them out as helper lemmas. This colocates proof obligations with the construction, matching Mathlib's house style for one-shot structure builders.
